data.title: Atomic Minerals announces option agreement to acquire Saskatchewan mineral claims
data.description:
• Atomic Minerals (TSXV:ATOM:CA) announced on Thursday that it has entered into an option agreement amongst the legal and beneficial holder of two mineral claims totaling approximately 5,355 hectares located in the province of Saskatchewan.
  • Pursuant to the option agreement, the vendor has granted the company the exclusive right to acquire a 100% interest in the property.
  • To exercise the option and acquire a 100% interest in the property, the company must issue 5.5 million common shares to the vendor and pay $50,000 in cash to the vendor within three years from the effective date of the option agreement.
  • The company also has the right to purchase a 3% net smelter return royalty held by an arm's-length third party by issuing 5.5 million consideration shares to the royalty holder.
  • The Mozzie Lake project consists of two properties situated in the Pinkham Lake Zone approximately 25km northeast of the Athabasca Basin, located within the Charlebois-Higgingson Lake Uranium District.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lazard expects lower AUM as of March end
data.description:
Financial advisory and asset management firm Lazard (NYSE:LAZ) announced on Thursday preliminary AUM of ~$227.42B, down from $232.88B at the end of the prior month.

Shares were trading +4.99% pre-market at $40.36.

March AUM takes into account a market depreciation of $5.4B, net outflows of $3.1B, and foreign exchange appreciation of $3.1B.

Sector-wise, Equity AUM is expected at $174.13B, Fixed Income at $44.02B, and Other at $9.27B.

data.title: Main Street Capital originates $130M new or increased commitments in Q1
data.description:
Main Street Capital (NYSE:MAIN) originated new or increased commitments in its private loan portfolio of $130.4M and funded total investments across its private loan portfolio with a cost basis totaling $138.2M, the company said on Thursday. That's an increase from the $123.4M in new or increased commitments in Q4 2024.

As of March 31, 2025, the company's private loan portfolio included total investments at a cost of ~$2.0B across 90 different companies. That's roughly the same total cost as of Dec. 31, 2024, which was across 91 unique borrowers.

The private loan portfolio, as a percentage of cost, included 94.7% invested in first lien debt investments and 5.3% invested in equity investments or other securities.

Among the larger commitments in Q1 2025 were:

  • $55.4M in first lien senior secured loan, $9.6M in a first lien senior revolver, and $1.5M in equity in a designer and manufacturer of engineered electrical power distribution solutions to datacenters and other power applications;
  • Increased commitment of $9.6M in incremental first lien senior secured loan, $2.4M in an incremental first lien senior secured revolver, and $0.4M in incremental equity to a provider of highly technical specialty services to datacenters and various other diverse end markets.

Main Street Capital (NYSE:MAIN) stock slipped 0.5% in Thursday premarket trading.

data.title: CarMax GAAP EPS of $0.58 misses by $0.08, revenue of $6B beats by $30M
data.description:
• CarMax press release (NYSE:KMX): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.58 misses by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $6B beats by $30M.
  • Retail used unit sales increased 6.2% and comparable store used unit sales increased 5.1%; wholesale units increased 3.1%.
  • Total gross profit of $667.9 million increased 13.9%, driven by unit volumes and strong unit margin performance.
  • Repurchased $98.5 million in shares of common stock in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2025.
  •

data.title: Mobilicom climbs 6%, secures scale orders from Asia
data.description:
Mobilicom (NASDAQ:MOB) received initial production-scale orders from a Tier-1 Asian conglomerate and manufacturer of robotics platforms that are sold to customers in the military and industrial markets.

The orders consisted of products from three of Mobilicom’s product families, including Mobilicom’s MCU Mesh Networking, SkyHopper Datalinks, and Mobile Ground Stations.

“While our strength to date has been sales in the U.S., Europe, and Israel, we are now experiencing increased commercial traction in Asia. These recent orders by a Tier-1 robotics manufacturer also demonstrate new momentum for Mobilicom in robotics, expanding beyond our well-established track record with drones.” said CEO and Founder Oren Elkayam. 

The stock price jumped 6.3% on Thursday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Polestar’s Q1 retail sales up 76% Y/Y
data.description:
Polestar (NASDAQ:PSNY) retail sales volumes amounted to an estimated 12,304 cars in the first quarter of 2025, up 76% compared to the same quarter last year and stable versus Q4 2024.

Sales are supported by a growing uptake of newer models and the ongoing transition to an active selling model, the Swedish electric vehicle maker said.

Polestar expects to publish its full-year results for 2024 and file its annual report on Form 20-F, by the end of April 2025.

Shares fell 4.1% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: Canaan reports increased bitcoin mining in March
data.description:
• Canaan (NASDAQ:CAN) mined 90 bitcoins (BTC-USD) in March, representing an increase of 9.8% compared to February.
  • The firm's month-end operating hashrate stood at 5.97 EH/s, while its average all-in power cost came in at $0.043/kWh.
  • The company also expanded its mining operations in North America, adding 0.32 exahash per second (EH/s) of installed computing power.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Virtus Investment Partners estimates lower AUM as of March end
data.description:
Virtus Investment Partners (NYSE:VRTS) announced on Thursday preliminary AUM of $167.46B as of March 31, down from $174.53B at February-end and $175.00 at the end of December 2024.

The decrease was primarily attributed to market performance and net outflows in U.S. retail mutual funds, institutional accounts and retail separate accounts.

By product type, Open-End Funds had AUM of $53.61B at the end of March, Closed-End Funds of $10.27B, Retail Separate Accounts of $46.92B, and Institutional Accounts of $56.66B.

data.title: Climb Channel Solutions partners with cybersecurity company
Piedmont Lithium to become Elevra Lithium upon merger completion with Sayona Mining
data.description:
Piedmont Lithium (NASDAQ:PLL) said Thursday it will rebrand as Elevra Lithium following the completion of its planned merger with Sayona Mining (OTCQB:SYAXF), which was announced in November.

Piedmont (NASDAQ:PLL) and Sayona (OTCQB:SYAXF) have selected their respective nominees to Elevra Lithium's board, which will consist of four from Piedmont's current board - including Dawne Hickton, the Chair designate of Elevra Lithium - and four from Sayona's board - including Lucas Dow, who will become Elevra's Managing Director and CEO.

Current Piedmont (PLL) CEO Keith Phillips will retire and become a strategic advisor to Elevra Lithium for a transition period.

The companies said the transaction is targeted to close in mid-2025 and remains subject to approval by Piedmont (PLL) and Sayona (OTCQB:SYAXF) shareholders, as well as regulatory approvals.
